item.title: AGA Clinical Practice Update on Management of Clostridioides difficile Infection in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Expert Review.

item.title: The missing evidence: Stricturing Crohn's disease in clinical trials, a systematic review.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Stricturing Crohn's disease (CD) represents a major cause of morbidity, with limited prospective data addressing response to treatment. We performed a systematic review to evaluate the efficacy of ...

item.title: Needles for healing: stem cells, platelet-enriched plasma, and fat injection in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Review - Perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease affects a third of patients with Crohn's disease and represents one of the most challenging complications to manage. This severe phenotype is characterized by a...

item.title: Fistulotomy for superficial or minimal sphincter-involving fistulae in perianal Crohn's disease: do they heal?
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Fistulotomy is highly effective (> 90%) for cryptoglandular fistula-on-ano, but fistulotomy in perianal Crohn's disease (pCD) is limited due to increased risk of recurrent fistulae, diarrhea, and f...

item.title: Roles for specialized secretory epithelial cells in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Current opinion in gastroenterology - Current inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) therapies mainly target immune pathways, yet many patients fail to achieve durable remission, highlighting the need to understand epithelial-intrinsic drive...

item.title: Advancement flaps in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease: technical steps and current evidence.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Review - Fistulizing perianal Crohn's disease is complex and negatively affects quality of life. Rectal advancement flap (AF) has been widely adopted as a sphincter-preserving procedure for treating complex...

item.title: Targeting fibroblasts in immune mediated inflammatory diseases: a cellular basis for cure?
item.description: Rheumatology (Oxford, England) - Fibroblasts constitute a major component of our stroma, the supportive bedding in which our tissues reside. The introduction of advanced single cell technologies has greatly enhanced our appreciati...

item.title: A gut vascular barrier damage-based scoring system predicts postoperative outcomes in Crohn's disease.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - The gut vascular barrier (GVB) is essential for maintaining intestinal homeostasis. However, its role in patients undergoing surgery for Crohn's disease (CD) remains unclear. Thus, our study aimed ...

item.title: Clostridioides difficile infection in inflammatory bowel disease: an observational study in flares of ulcerative colitis and a South Asian region-specific systematic review and meta-analysis.

item.title: Εsophageal Crohn's disease in adults: A systematic review of the literature.
item.description: Surgery - Review - Esophageal involvement in Crohn's disease is rare and poorly understood, presenting with nonspecific symptoms that contribute to delayed diagnosis and inconsistent management. This systematic revie...

item.title: Phenotype-driven management in ileocecal Crohn's disease: a narrative review with a proposed research framework.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Review - The management of localized ileocecal Crohn's disease (CD) requires balancing escalation of medical therapy against early surgical resection. While clinical phenotyping guides initial management, s...

item.title: Bayesian prior elicitation on the efficacy of medical therapies in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Robust evidence for most licensed Crohn's disease therapies is lacking for perianal fistula outcomes due to a lack of dedicated clinical trials. This study aimed to use a Bayesian framework to dete...

item.title: Hyperbaric oxygen therapy alters bowel perfusion and improves outcomes in patients with treatment-refractory ulcerative colitis: a prospective pilot trial.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT) delivers 100% oxygen in a pressurized chamber and enhances tissue oxygenation and neovascularization. While effective in radiation proctitis, acute severe ulcerativ...

item.title: How I Do It: MRI Approach to Perianal Fistulas.
item.description: Radiology - Review - Perianal fistula is a common, miserable, and often embarrassing condition that tends to recur. Most cases result from chronic infection of the anal glands (cryptoglandular), leading to an internal ...

item.title: Terminal Ileal Ulcers: Endoscopic Predictors for the Later Development of Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Diagnostics (Basel, Switzerland) - Background: Terminal ileal ulcers (TIUs) are frequently identified during colonoscopy but are often attributed to nonspecific etiologies, potentially delaying the diagnosis of Crohn's disease (CD)....

item.title: Obesity: An Overlooked Factor in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.

item.title: Severe Myenteric Plexitis at the Proximal Margin Predicts Postoperative Recurrence in Crohn's Disease Irrespective of Margin Distance.
item.description: Journal of the anus, rectum and colon - Postoperative clinical recurrence is common in patients with Crohn's disease (CD) despite intestinal resection. Although histopathological findings at resection margins suggestively influence recur...
